name: org.machanism.machai:machai-mcp-server
description: Machai MCP Server is a Java 17 executable server module for exposing Machai GenAI tools through the Model Context Protocol (MCP). It supports STDIO transport for process-based integrations and HTTP transport for remote MCP clients using Jetty, with both stateless and streamable session-aware HTTP modes. The artifact provides the command-line entry point org.machanism.machai.mcp.server.McpServer, transport-specific server implementations, shared server abstractions for project-directory and lifecycle handling, and GenericGenaiAdapter implementations that convert Machai ToolFunction definitions, prompts, resources, and parameter descriptors into MCP tool schemas and synchronous MCP tool specifications. It is intended for AI-powered tool execution, orchestration, MCP client integration, and publishing external functional-tool libraries through a standard MCP interface; it does not bundle domain tools itself and is designed to load additional Machai tool and prompt libraries via configuration.

usageContext: Run an MCP server over STDIO for local AI client integrations
Run an MCP server over HTTP for remote clients
Expose Machai GenAI ToolFunction implementations as MCP tools
Register prompts and resources for MCP-capable AI applications
Package and launch a Java command-line MCP server

constructors.description: Creates an HTTP stateless MCP server. The supplied name and version are reported through the MCP API, and tools can be loaded with tools(org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.Configurator) before starting the server.

examples.description: Creates a stateless HTTP MCP server, configures the listening port, and sets a project directory used by server tools. Call server.tools(config) with an org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.Configurator and then server.start() to serve MCP requests.

constructors.description: Creates an HTTP streamable MCP server for session-aware MCP communication. The server loads GenAI tools and exposes them over Jetty-backed HTTP transport.

examples.description: Creates a streamable HTTP MCP server for clients that need session-aware communication. Load tools with an org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.Configurator and start it with server.start().

constructors.description: Default constructor for the command-line application entry point. Most applications invoke the static main method rather than constructing this class directly.

examples.description: Invokes the MCP server entry point programmatically with a server name, HTTP port, and project directory. This mirrors command-line usage.

features.name: main
features.signature: public static void main(java.lang.String[] args) throws java.lang.Exception
features.description: Starts the MCP server application. The method parses command-line options and selects STDIO mode by default or HTTP remote mode when -p or --port is provided. Options include -h/--help, -n/--name, -d/--projectDir, -v/--version, -p/--port, and -s/--session for streamable HTTP mode.

examples.description: Runs the server in default STDIO mode for local MCP clients. The project directory is passed to tools that need filesystem context.
Runs the server as a remote stateless HTTP MCP server listening on port 8080.
Runs the server in streamable, session-aware HTTP mode. Use this when MCP clients require HTTP session-oriented communication.

features.name: setPort
features.description: Sets the HTTP listening port used by org.machanism.machai.mcp.server.AbstractHttpMcpServer subclasses before startup.

examples.description: Configures an HTTP MCP server to listen on port 9000 before calling start().

features.name: start
features.description: Starts an HTTP MCP server and binds the configured Jetty servlet transport so incoming MCP requests can be handled. Startup failures are propagated as java.lang.Exception.

examples.description: Starts a stateless HTTP MCP server on port 8080. In a real application, load tools with server.tools(config) before start().

features.name: tools
features.description: Loads and registers GenAI tools with an HTTP MCP server. The method uses FunctionToolsLoader and GenericGenaiAdapter to convert configured Machai tool definitions into MCP tools.

examples.description: Demonstrates where tool registration occurs. Replace the null placeholder with a real org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.Configurator implementation configured with Machai tools and prompts.

features.name: toHumanReadable
features.description: Converts a snake_case or camelCase function tool name to a human-readable title for MCP tool display metadata.

examples.description: Produces a readable label such as Do Something Cool from a tool function name.

features.name: buildSpecification
features.description: Builds a stateless MCP synchronous tool specification from a generic tool object, expected to be an io.modelcontextprotocol.spec.McpSchema.Tool, and a call handler for invoking the tool.

examples.description: Defines the handler shape required by the stateless tool specification builder. Supply a real io.modelcontextprotocol.spec.McpSchema.Tool object to buildSpecification together with this handler.

features.name: getProjectDir
features.description: Returns the configured project directory used by server tools, or null when the server should use request-specific directories.

examples.description: Stores and retrieves the project directory that tools can use for project-scoped operations.

customizations.name: Tool libraries and prompts loaded through configuration
customizations.description: The server is designed to be extended by adding libraries that implement Machai functional tools and prompts. These are loaded through org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.Configurator and applied to the MCP server by FunctionToolsLoader and GenericGenaiAdapter.

examples.description: Configure external Machai tool libraries, create a real Configurator, and pass it to tools(config) so the MCP server exposes those tools.

customizations.name: GenericGenaiAdapter
customizations.description: Adapter customization point for converting Machai ToolFunction definitions, parameter descriptors, prompts, and resources into transport-specific MCP specifications. Concrete variants exist for STDIO, stateless HTTP, and streamable HTTP.

examples.description: Shows use of shared adapter utility logic when preparing display metadata for custom tools.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.mcp.server.AbstractMcpServer
studs.description: Base implementation for MCP servers. It provides shared metadata constants and project-directory management for concrete server implementations such as STDIO and HTTP servers.

examples.description: Uses inherited project-directory configuration from org.machanism.machai.mcp.server.AbstractMcpServer through a concrete implementation.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.mcp.server.AbstractHttpMcpServer
studs.description: Base class for Jetty-backed HTTP MCP servers. It handles HTTP servlet transport provider setup, thread pool management, connector setup, port configuration, and common server startup behavior.

examples.description: Uses inherited HTTP port configuration from org.machanism.machai.mcp.server.AbstractHttpMcpServer.

examples.title: Install as a Maven dependency for embedding server classes
steps: Add the Maven dependency org.machanism.machai:machai-mcp-server:1.2.2-SNAPSHOT to a Java 17 project.
Add or configure additional Machai tool libraries that provide functional tools and prompts.
Create an HTTP server implementation, set its port and project directory, load tools through a Configurator, then start the server.
examples.description: Use this dependency when an application needs to create and configure org.machanism.machai.mcp.server.HttpStatelessMcpServer or org.machanism.machai.mcp.server.HttpStreamableMcpServer directly.

examples.title: Package and run the ready-to-use CLI MCP server
steps: Build the executable jar with the Maven pack profile so the org.machanism.machai.mcp.server.McpServer main class is written to the manifest.
Choose STDIO mode by omitting --port, or choose HTTP mode by passing --port.
Pass --projectDir so tools can operate against the intended project workspace.
Use --session with --port when a streamable, session-aware HTTP MCP server is required.
examples.configure: Provide command-line options such as --name, --version, --projectDir, --port, and --session. Configure external Machai tool libraries separately so the server has tools to expose.
examples.description: This scenario demonstrates installation, configuration, and invocation of the ready-to-use MCP server component in real deployments.

examples.title: Programmatically start a stateless HTTP MCP server
examples.description: Embed the server in a Java process. Replace the null Configurator with a real org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.Configurator that knows about the Machai tools and prompts to expose.
